text: The robotic side cart is positioned to the left of the patient at the foot of the bed. The surgical team utilizes a three-arm robotic technique with an assistant 5mm port. A 12mm camera trocar is centered between two 8mm robotic trocars.

The surgeon reduces the small bowel incarcerated in the hernia, taking down adhesions to mobilize the bowel further. The hernia's extent is fully visualized, and the left ureter with a lighted stent and bilateral iliac vessels are identified. Preperitoneal dissection begins on the right side of the pelvic wall.

The surgeon continues the dissection anteriorly towards the patient's left side, creating a peritoneal flap. The flap is positioned over the hernia defect to obliterate the space and act as a suspensory membrane for the mesh. Interrupted absorbable sutures are used to secure the flap in place.

The surgeon measures the space and introduces a coated polypropylene mesh marked at the midline. After identifying the sacral promontory, the mesh is anchored with non-absorbable sutures. The mesh is then fixated to the pelvic sidewall, avoiding the ureter and iliac vessels, and is also secured to Cooper's ligaments bilaterally.
The surgeon completes the final fixation of the mesh to the median umbilical ligament using an absorbable suture.
